1.18 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) to
effectively facilitate the conduct of actual physical count of PPEs in
compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. Personnel concerned to complete the physical count of PPE and consider
the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No.
2020-006;
c. Municipal Accountant and Storekeeper to update and prepare the
PPELC and PC, respectively, for each class of PPE; and
d. Accounting Office and General Services Office to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their PPE records and reconcile their records with the
RPCCPE and to immediately effect necessary adjustments, if any.

2.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the concerned
personnel to coordinate and follow-up with the PCSO on the PTV donation
documents.

2.10 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entry to record the cost of the PTV based on the fair value
as at the date of acquisition and provide for the corresponding depreciation
thereof.

3.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer to
establish the respective shares of the Municipality and Barangays out of
the RPT collections by the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO)
amounting to ₱1.994 million;
b. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts of the Municipality’s RPT share once the amount is established;
c. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer to the
respective Barangays their RPT shares;
d. Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries for the
Barangays’ counterpart and receipt of cash awards/incentives to correct
the balance of the Due to LGUs account; and
e. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that RPT collections
made by the PTO are broken down into Municipality and Barangay
shares to facilitate proper recording of the transactions in the books by
the Municipal Accountant.

4.7 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to clearly identify and classify the type of expense and to record
the amount to the appropriate account in conformity with COA Circular No.
2015-009, to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.

5.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that progress billings for infrastructure or capital
outlay projects are recorded under the Construction in Progress (CIP)
account, in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 1, the New Government
Accounting System (NGAS) Manual, Volume I, and COA Circular Nos. 2015-
009 and 2012-002.

5.11 Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed that
necessary adjusting entries be prepared to correct the erroneous recording of
₱0.691 million to the Trust Liabilities – DRRM account, to ensure that both
the Trust Liabilities and CIP accounts are fairly presented in the financial
statements and aligned with applicable government accounting standards.

6.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office on the status
of physical accomplishments of the foregoing infrastructure project of the
Municipality and to facilitate adjustments of the CIP and Inventory accounts
equivalent to the amount reflective of their actual statuses.

6.12 Also, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
concerned offices to strictly adhere to the prescribed procedures on the
requisition, issuance, and recording of construction supplies/materials, in
accordance with government accounting and supply management guidelines.

6.13 Lastly, we recommended and the Management agreed to establish a proper
inventory management system to ensure adequate documentation and accurate
recording of supplies/inventories in the books and to avoid future
misstatements in the financial records.

7.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with DARPO Iloilo for the
reconciliation of records and make proper adjustments in the
Municipality’s books of accounts.
b. Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund to the source agency the
excess or unutilized balances as soon as the reconciliation of records has
been completed by the Municipal Accountant.

8.8 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to regularly prepare the certified list of RPT and SET
due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant at the beginning of the year for the setting up of receivables.
b. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the RPT and SET
receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list of due
and collectible real property taxes as submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer.

9.8	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and School Heads to:
Submit the job order contract with a stipulation as to the rate/salary  of the Job Hires to support the payment of ₱527,000.00; and
Henceforth, see to it that Job Order Contract is sufficient in content and consistent with Item 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 so that compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445   is assured.

10.6	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Instruct the MENRO-Designate to expedite the processing of the required permit and certification for the continued operation of the ultra-high temperature pyrolysis system equipment to maximize its usage and to ensure that its harmless effects on the environment is assured.
Ensure that future projects are properly planned and implementation ready to avoid wastage of public funds.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to verify the validity of payables aged two years and more and send confirmation letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors. Valid claims shall be paid. Otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials agreed to: 

a.	Intensify efforts to enforce collection remedies provided by Republic Act No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in order to demonstrate the Municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions; and

b.	Conduct more extensive tax campaigns so that taxpayers are apprised of the advantages/services the Municipality could render if taxes are paid.

We recommended and the Management agreed that in the preparation of the updated Annual Investment Program, LDRRMFIP and Annual Procurement Plan, the Municipality should comply with the necessary planning and reporting requirements and in the prescribed format in accordance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124, DILG DBM Circular No. 02, COA Circular No. 2012-02 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 as the basis for procurement activities of COVID-19 PPAs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer to:

a. Assess the activities to be included LDRRMIP and determine if the charges against the LDRRMF are appropriate; and

b..Ensure that the four disaster risk reduction and management thematic areas namely, disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness and response activities and equipment are prioritized.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the SWM Board and MENRO to:

Monitor the implementation and effectiveness of the ESWMP in order that measures to correct the deficiencies noted will be timely instituted; and

Revisit the Republic Act 9003 and comply with the provisions of Sections 12 and 17 of the aforementioned law.

Ensure that Purchase Requests, Purchase Orders and Requests for Quotations for meals are duly supported with food specifications and payments for meals are based on the actual number of event attendees; and

Submit Purchase Orders within the prescribed period, as well as the Acceptance and Inspection Reports of consumable and perishable items to this Office within 24 hours from acceptance of the items delivered.

Recommendations

Create a Disposal Committee tasked with the disposal of unserviceable properties;
Direct the OIC-General Services Officer to identify the unserviceable properties subject to inspection and appraisal in order to expedite their disposal; and
Direct the Disposal Committee to follow the disposal procedures and accomplish the forms to be used in reporting disposable supplies and property.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials agreed to:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee - Technical Working Group to extensively evaluate during post-qualification the POW and detailed Bill of Materials and Estimates of Bidders in comparison with that of the Municipality and in accordance with DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2006; and
Instruct the winning Bidder/Contractor to refund the excessive amount collected on awarded contract amounting to ₱40,701.34 pursuant to DPWH DO No. 197 series of 2006, otherwise, the same will be shouldered by the BAC and approving officers.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned officials to:
Submit the supplier’s FDA Special Certification as well as certification as authorized distributor; otherwise, submit a justification as to why the award was given to the supplier despite the non-submission of the said documentary requirement.
Provide the details or necessary information in the supporting documents inclusive of Purchase Request and Purchase Order as provided in Section 18 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 96-010, respectively.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Require the concerned and retired elected officials and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances. Demand letters should be sent periodically to concerned officials to immediately liquidate their cash advances, otherwise, withholding of the payment of salaries of accountable officers shall be made in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Stop granting additional cash advances with the same purpose/s to the concerned Disbursing Officer, Special Disbursing Officers, employees and elected officials unless their previous cash advances are settled and proper accounting thereof are made.
Refrain from granting cash advances to elected employees two months prior to end/term of office and require payment of unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, the same shall be deducted from their salaries or clearance shall not be issued thereof.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to notify the concerned accountable officers that failure to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribe period are subject to imposition of administrative liability and corresponding penalties as embodied per CSC MC No. 23, series of 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and officials concerned ensure to:

a. Direct the Inventory Committee to establish the existence, condition, validity, and accuracy of its fixed assets by conducting a physical inventory in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006, and Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 which prescribes the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the LGUs;

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to maintain subsidiary ledger cards and Property Cards for PPE, respectively; and



c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to reconcile the accounting and property records on PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain the cashbooks for all cash accounts effective upon his assumption to the position and henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation of Cash in bank balances per cashbook and per books maintained by the Municipal Accountant in accordance with the Revised Cash Examination Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to deposit his collections intact or in full amount as received daily or on the next banking day to the authorized depository bank and to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of PD 1445. Monthly reconciliation of cashbook balances should be conducted with that of the accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

Municipal Engineer to prioritize the implementation of projects programmed under the 20% DF that yield greater benefits to the constituents in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1; and

Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Budget Officer, and Municipal Engineer to utilize the unexpended balance of appropriated funds for the intended purpose after due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns taking into consideration current developments and to accordingly re-appropriate the funds of projects which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority undertaking.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned GSO-Designate to issue Inventory Custodian Slip to tangible items costing less than ₱15,000.00 and has a life of more than one year and are not considered as PPE to establish monitoring, control, and accountability over them.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Disaster Risk Management Officer to prepare and submit the annual LDRRMFIP to include the current year and unexpended balance of the previous years’ LDRRMF  to the Office of the Auditor duly received by the MDRRMC, OCD and the DILG-LGOO so that the funds will be properly monitored and appropriately used for the intended purpose.

observations.recommendation:
a. Direct   the   inventory team to conduct inventory and submit inventory reports on the prescribed format. The results of the inventory-taking should be reconciled with accounting records in order to present fairly the PPE account as presented in the financial statements.

b.  Instruct  the  Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Cash in Treasury account balance as of December 31, 2019 with that of the cash books and any difference shall be accounted for by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the LDRRM Officer to assess the adequacy and availability of basic rescue and response equipment in the LDRRM Office and to prioritize the procurement of early warning systems, search, rescue and retrieval equipment and other equipage useful for disaster preparedness and disaster response.

b. .Direct the LDRRM Officer to revisit DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and accounting and reporting guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to:

Prepare a LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, and rehabilitation and recovery pursuant to Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.
	
Submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in the prescribed format in accordance with Annex B to the audit team for review and verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

The Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to discontinue the practice of charging prior years’ expenditure to current year appropriations and that proper matching of expenses against appropriation be always observed pursuant to the above-cited regulations.

The Municipal Accountant to evaluate the nature and effect of the transactions, determine the necessary adjusting entries and record the same in the books to reflect the correct balances of the accounts. She should see to it that expenses incurred but not paid in prior years must be recorded in the books as accounts payable.

The submission of an appropriation ordinance enacting a supplemental budget for the purpose of charging prior years’ expenditures in the CY 2019 appropriations.

The BAC Chairperson and Members to adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184 on all procurement activities of the Municipality and ensure that the supporting documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement voucher.

The Municipal Accountant to withhold the required taxes from payment thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned officers and employees to liquidate their long outstanding cash advances. Demand letters should be sent to concerned officials and employees to immediately liquidate their cash advances.



We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Accountant stop granting additional cash advances to the concerned accountable officers unless their previous cash advances are settled and proper accounting thereof are made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:

Indicate in the POW the specific scope of work with the corresponding estimated labor cost, the target date to be started and completed, the specific location of the project and other important details required in the POW.	

Implement the priority projects of the Municipality strictly in accordance with the POW and the detailed Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates. Any deviation thereof, shall be indicated in a Revised POW and corresponding Bill of Materials and Cost Estimates in order that actual implementation of the project could be monitored.

Conduct regular monitoring of priority projects being implemented by the Municipality through contract or by administration.

Require the Contractor to refund the amounts equivalent to the provision of project billboard and to undertake the repair of holes and cracks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the BAC Chairperson and Members to impose penalty of suspension and/or blacklisting of contractors and suppliers having a track record of liquidated damages pursuant to Section 69 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 and Appendix 17 issued by the GPPB.

Instruct the Municipal Engineer in her capacity as BAC Member to assist in the determination and evaluation of the cause/s of delay in project implementation by the contractors and suppliers so that immediate appropriate actions could be addressed by ensuring that the projects are implemented in accordance with the target dates of completion.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit their GAD Plan and Budget to PPDO and prepare the accomplishment report based on DILG endorsed GPB or LGU budget and submit the same to the audit team for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and record adjustments for valid reconciling items as reflected in the BRS. The BRS submitted to the Auditor should be supported by copies of the JEV recording the reconciling items that require correction in the books of accounts, as well as a copy of the letter to the respective banks informing them of errors (if any) noted in the BRS. Prior to actual reconciliation, verification of supporting paid checks, debit memos and credit memos should be made upon receipt of the bank statements,  to ensure that debits and credits made in the bank statements are properly documented by the Bank.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant effort will be exerted in determining the unreconciled credit memo, and other reconciling items, and properly label the items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements. Cash balances and transactions should be monitored regularly to determine proper use of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Implement the prompt and proper liquidation of funds received from Source Agencies, including the return of excess/unutilized amount.

Reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the Source Agencies and prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and official concerned strictly enforce Section 21 of RA 9003 and create Solid Waste regulations to enable proper segregation of solid waste for the full cooperation of the residents, institutions, industrial and agricultural sectors. He should further focus on the sanitary landfill since the proposal of Passi City as the common sanitary land has not yet materialized.
 
We further recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan propose revenue generating measures for approval of SWM Board pursuant to Section 12 (4) of Republic Act 9003 in order to ensure the viability of its 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan. 

We also recommended that the SWM Board strengthen the Information, Education and Communication (IEC) campaign so that its constituents will be aware of the present solid waste management condition of the municipality and be able to actively participate in the SWM programs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reorganize the created Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all the Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the municipality and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.



We likewise recommended that the Accounting Office maintain the prescribed PPELC which should be reconciled with the RPCPPE to establish the correctness of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the bank reconciliation statements for GF-LBP and SEF-LBP for December 2017; November to December 2017 for TF-LBP accounts to comply with Section 74 of PD 1445 and to prove the correctness and existence of the cash in bank balances at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to prioritize the procurement of early warning systems, search, rescue and retrieval equipment and other equipage useful for disaster preparedness and disaster response other than administrative expenses and see to it that charges to LDRRMF are in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We further recommended that the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant submit the LDRRM Plan for CY 2018 and the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and that projects and activities charged against the LDRRMF shall be incorporated in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan (LDRRMP) and integrated in the approve Annual Investment Program (AIP) of the LGU.

To strictly comply with applicable laws, rules and regulations relative to disbursement that are considered irregular expenditures so as to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to submit their GAD Plan and Budget to PPDO and prepare the accomplishment report based on DILG endorsed GPB or LGU budget and submit the same to the audit team for verification.
